buddies.name: Linh Nguyen
buddies.description: Art historian and street food expert. Let me show you the hidden alleys and 1000-year history of Hanoi's Old Quarter.

buddies.name: Nam Tran
buddies.description: Coffee enthusiast and architecture buff. From secret rooftop bars to colonial heritage, experience the vibrant energy of Saigon.

buddies.name: Minh Anh
buddies.description: Born and raised in the ancient capital. Specialized in imperial cuisine and the poetic beauty of the Perfume River.

buddies.name: Hoang Nam
buddies.description: Surfer and photography enthusiast. Let's explore the Marble Mountains and the stunning coastal roads of Da Nang.

buddies.name: An Nhien
buddies.description: Culinary student with a passion for traditional flavors. Join me for a journey through Hanoi's best Pho spots and hidden street food gems.

buddies.name: Quoc Bao
buddies.description: Professional travel photographer. I'll take you to the most cinematic spots in Da Nang, including the iconic Golden Bridge.

reviews.author: James Wilson
reviews.content: An incredible guide with deep knowledge of local history. The best part was the hidden cafes we visited and the authentic stories shared.

reviews.author: Hoang An
reviews.content: Quoc Bao was an amazing buddy! We had a great time exploring the city together. Highly recommended.

buddies.name: Thuy Tien
buddies.description: Nature lover and trekking guide. Discover the emerald beauty of Sapa's terraced fields and the unique culture of ethnic minorities.

buddies.name: Minh Nhat
buddies.description: History buff and heritage conservationist. Dive deep into the imperial history of Hue at the Citadel and royal tombs.

buddies.name: Mock Buddy
buddies.description: New Local Buddy joined our community!

buddies.name: demo123
buddies.description: New Local Buddy joined our community!

matches.snippet: Hey! I'd love to take you to the best Pho place in Hanoi...
Saigon nightlife is amazing, shall we start at District 1?

conversations.name: Hoang An
conversations.lastMsg: That sounds perfect! I'll book it now.

messages.text: Hi Linh! I'm planning to visit Hanoi Old Quarter on the 24th. Are you available?
Hello Hoang An! Yes, I'm absolutely available. I'd love to show you the best street food spots!
Awesome! How long does the food tour usually take?
Usually around 3 to 4 hours. We can customize it based on what you want to eat. I know a great hidden spot for egg coffee as well.
Custom Offer: 4 Hours of Food Tour & Egg Coffee
That sounds perfect! I'll book it now.

conversations.name: David Miller
conversations.lastMsg: What kind of beer do we try?

requests.message: Hi Linh! We are visiting Vietnam for the first time and really want to experience the authentic local life that isn't in guidebooks.

notifications.title: Order Confirmation
notifications.desc: Buddy Linh Nguyen has sent you an offer for a 3-hour exploration. Tap to pay now!

notifications.title: Booking Reminder
notifications.desc: Your trip with Nam Tran starts in 1 hour. Don't forget the meeting point at Hoan Kiem Lake!

notifications.title: Journey Started
notifications.desc: Minh Anh has started the trip. Have a wonderful experience!

notifications.title: Time Warning
notifications.desc: Your trip has 15 minutes left. Would you like to extend for more time?

notifications.title: Review & Share
notifications.desc: The trip has ended! How was Quoc Bao? Share your experience to earn bonus points!

users.name: Hoang An
users.description: I'm a local food enthusiast and history lover. I've lived in Hoi An for most of my life and love sharing the hidden charms of this ancient town with travelers.

users.name: Linh Nguyen
users.description: Art historian and street food expert. Let me show you the hidden alleys and 1000-year history of Hanoi's Old Quarter.

users.name: demo123
users.description: New Local Buddy joined our community!

experiences.title: Magical Evening in Hoi An
experiences.storyContent: I never thought making a lantern could be so therapeutic! Quoc Bao (my buddy) showed me the hidden workshop of a master craftsman. We spent hours talking about Hoi An's history over tea while the silk took shape. It wasn't just a tour; it was a real connection to the soul of the city. If you're in Hoi An, you HAVE to find Quoc Bao!

experiences.title: Hidden Flavors of the Old Quarter
experiences.storyContent: Hanoi's street food can be overwhelming, but Sarah (yes, we have the same name!) made it feel like a dinner party with an old friend. We skipped the 'tourist' places and sat on tiny plastic stools for the best Bun Cha I've ever tasted. Her stories about growing up in these alleys made every bite meaningful. Best decision ever!

experiences.title: Morning Market & Coffee Secrets
experiences.storyContent: Linh took me to the early morning market before the sun was even fully up. Seeing the city wake up and learning about the ingredients used in traditional Hanoi dishes was eye-opening. We finished with the most incredible egg coffee in a hidden courtyard. She's not just a guide, she's like a local sister showing you her home.

experiences.title: Breathtaking Sunset over the Bay
experiences.storyContent: Watching the sun dip below the karsts of Ha Long was peak existence. My buddy Minh Nhat knew exactly when and where to go to avoid the massive cruise ship crowds. We had a small private boat, cold drinks, and perfect silence. His local knowledge about the bay's formations was incredible.

experiences.title: Saigon Coffee Culture Exploration
experiences.storyContent: Nam showed me the most amazing hidden coffee spots in Saigon. From apartment cafes to street-side drip coffee, every stop had a story. His knowledge of the city's architecture and history made the tour so much more than just about coffee.

experiences.title: Sapa Terraced Fields Trekking
experiences.storyContent: Thuy Tien is an amazing guide! She took us on a trek through the most beautiful terraced fields I've ever seen. We visited local ethnic minority villages and learned so much about their culture. A truly authentic experience.
